subject: Accounting
title: The Current Ratio
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Current Ratio.

hook.text: Can this company pay its bills for the next twelve months? One ratio answers that fast.

buildup.text: The current ratio divides current assets by current liabilities.

discovery.text: A ratio above 1.0 means more short-term assets than debts. Below 1.0 signals potential trouble.

twist.text: A ratio too high might mean the company is hoarding cash instead of investing it.

climax.text: Different industries have different norms. A 1.5 might be great for retail but low for utilities.

punchline.text: Can you pay your bills? The current ratio knows.

quiz.question: What does a current ratio below 1.0 indicate?
